title: At 22, she’s running Boston for her mom — and a future without Alzheimer’s
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Ella Wright, 22, is running the Boston Marathon to honor her mother, who was diagnosed with early-onset Alzheimer's.
Wright's family has been heavily impacted by Alzheimer's, having lost multiple relatives to the disease.
She aims to raise awareness and funding for Alzheimer's research, running for the dream her mom couldn't fulfill.

title: Plymouth officer charged with child rape suspended by POST Commission
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Plymouth Police Officer Samantha Pelrine suspended by POST Commission after child rape charges.
Pelrine and husband Daniel Forand accused of sexually abusing a boy between 2018 and 2025.
Couple posted bail and are due back in court on June 8, ordered no contact with minors.

title: After 7 e-bike incidents in 10 days, Mass. police chief warns of impending ‘tragedy’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Milford police reported seven e-bike incidents in 10 days, prompting safety warnings from Chief Robert Tusino.
A 15-year-old lost control of an e-bike and collided with a car at Highlight Street; no injuries occurred.
Massachusetts e-bike laws require riders to be 16, with helmets mandatory, amid rising safety concerns statewide.

title: Bridgewater police warn of Tom Brady impersonation scam
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Bridgewater police warn of scam with impersonator posing as Tom Brady defrauding residents using gift cards.
A local family reported the scam on March 23, prompting police to provide guidance to prevent further losses.
The Federal Trade Commission categorizes these impersonation tactics as 'imposter scams', urging vigilance against unsolicited requests.

title: This Boston Marathon runner is supporting children in their hardest times
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Maria Castaneda, 52, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support The Children's Room.
The Children's Room provides support to children and families dealing with grief and difficult times.
Castaneda expresses gratitude to Boston's community, highlighting their kindness since she moved from Mexico.

title: Review and setlist: Lady Gaga’s Mayhem Ball brought the spectacle, but Boston brought the heart
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lady Gaga's Mayhem Ball at TD Garden featured gothic theatrics and audience tributes that reduced her to tears.
The three-hour show included songs from her new album 'Mayhem' and classics like 'Poker Face' and 'Born This Way.'
Fans coordinated a surprise tribute for Gaga's 40th birthday, displaying signs with the message 'another year happy just to be alive.'

title: Would you try the new couch-style seating on United? Here’s what readers said.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines plans to introduce Relax Row couch-style seating in 2027, transforming three economy seats into a couch.
A Boston.com poll shows 61% of readers are eager to try the new seating concept, while 15% oppose it.
Concerns include potential misbehavior, noise, cost, and practicality during flights, especially with safety considerations.

title: This first grade teacher is running the Boston Marathon to support her students
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Siobhan Salani, a teacher from Weymouth, is running the Boston Marathon for the Corey C. Griffin Foundation.
The foundation supports students at Saint John Paul II Catholic Academy Lower Mills, where Salani teaches first grade.
Salani feels honored to run, highlighting the foundation's positive impact on her students and Boston's children.

title: A Maine beach is among the best in the U.S. in 2026, according to Conde Nast Traveler
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Sand Beach at Acadia National Park in Maine is among Conde Nast Traveler's best U.S. beaches for 2026.
Sand Beach features pale shell sand, pine-covered cliffs, and cold Atlantic water, ideal for quiet, nature-filled walks.
Recommended nearby accommodations include Under Canvas Acadia and The Asticou Hotel, highlighted by Vogue and Thrillist.

title: 16-year-old arrested in Allston shooting
author: John Waller
bullets: A 16-year-old boy was arrested for a shooting in Allston that injured another juvenile last Thursday.
The teenager faces multiple charges, including assault with intent to murder and carrying a loaded firearm without a license.
The incident occurred near 222 Brighton Ave. in Allston, and the victim's injuries were not life-threatening.
